Chwang mentions it as a country which lay in the Garhwal and
Kumaun region.
Darva is closely associated with Abhisara and these together
roughly comprised the Punch and Naushera region between the
Jhelum and the Chenab.
Dfimaras are identified by Wilson with a fierce intractable
tribe inhabiting the mountains to the north of Kashmir.
The Cinarattha is identified with the Himavantapadesa in the
Pali Sasanaoaihsa. B.C. Law places it in the Himalayas beyond
Cilata or Kir^ta. Kern translates it into 'the Chinese*.
Pa{ola according to Kern, is Palola, and a corruption of the
i
word Palvala. He suggests thai by Palola is meant the eastern part
of the Tarai near Gooch-Bihar.
SuoarnabhU is sometimes identified with Ptolemy's Golden
Khersonesc or the delta of the Irawadi forming the province of
Pegu. But it may include also Malaysia and the islands of eastern
archipelago.
